fields.caption: Cal Int Diff +
fields.tableau_formula:
IF [Latest BMI (copy) (copy) (copy) (copy)_549157724182474763] > [Latest Cal Int (copy)_549157724212244504]
THEN [Latest BMI (copy) (copy) (copy) (copy)_549157724182474763] - [Latest Cal Int (copy)_549157724212244504]
END

fields.caption: Steps Count Diff +
fields.tableau_formula:

THEN [Latest HR bpm (copy) (copy)_549157724183281679] - [Latest Steps Count (copy)_549157724212244510]
END

fields.caption: Sleep Duration Diff +
fields.tableau_formula:

THEN [Latest HR bpm (copy)_549157724183273486] - [Latest Sleep Dur (copy)_549157724212244511]
END

fields.caption: Mood Score Diff +
fields.tableau_formula:
IF [Latest BMI (copy) (copy)_549157724183068685] > [Latest Mood Score (copy)_549157724212244507]
THEN [Latest BMI (copy) (copy)_549157724183068685] - [Latest Mood Score (copy)_549157724212244507]
END

fields.caption: BP text
fields.tableau_formula:
IF [Latest BMI (copy) (copy)_549157724182454281] <= 120 AND [Latest BMI (copy)_549157724182437896] <= 80 THEN
    'Normal Blood Pressure'
ELSEIF [Latest BMI (copy) (copy)_549157724182454281] <= 129 AND [Latest BMI (copy)_549157724182437896] < 80 THEN
    'Elevated Blood Pressure'
ELSEIF ([Latest BMI (copy) (copy)_549157724182454281] <= 139 AND [Latest BMI (copy) (copy)_549157724182454281] >= 130) OR ([Latest BMI (copy)_549157724182437896] <= 89 AND [Latest BMI (copy)_549157724182437896] >= 80) THEN
    'Stage 1 Hypertension'
ELSEIF ([Latest BMI (copy) (copy)_549157724182454281] <= 180 AND [Latest BMI (copy) (copy)_549157724182454281] >= 140) OR ([Latest BMI (copy)_549157724182437896] <= 120 AND [Latest BMI (copy)_549157724182437896] >= 90) THEN
    'Stage 2 Hypertension'
ELSE
    'Hypertensive Crisis - Consult a Doctor Immediately'
END

fields.caption: Water Intake Diff +
fields.tableau_formula:
IF [Latest Sleep Dur (copy)_549157724183420944] > [Latest Water Intake (copy)_549157724212244512]
THEN [Latest Sleep Dur (copy)_549157724183420944] - [Latest Water Intake (copy)_549157724212244512]
END

fields.caption: Cal Bur Diff -
fields.tableau_formula:

THEN [Latest BMI (copy) (copy) (copy)_549157724182466570] - [Latest Cal Bur (copy)_549157724212244505]
END

fields.tableau_formula:

END

fields.caption: Date period selected
fields.tableau_formula:

        WHEN 'Last Week' THEN DATENAME('day', [Date])
        WHEN 'Last Month' THEN DATENAME('day', [Date])
        WHEN 'Last Year' THEN DATENAME('month', [Date])
END

fields.caption: Date Color
fields.tableau_formula:
CASE [Parameters].[Parameter 1]
    WHEN 'Last Week' THEN [Calculation_120752809788018723]
    WHEN 'Last Month' THEN [Calculation_120752809788018723]
    WHEN 'Last Year' THEN [LC Week (copy)_549157724169756674]
END

fields.caption: Latest Day
fields.tableau_formula: DATETRUNC('day', [Date]) = DATEADD('day', -1, DATETRUNC('day', { MAX([Date])} + 1))

fields.caption: Label
fields.tableau_formula:
IF [Calculation_120752809787707426] = TRUE
THEN 'Yes'
END

fields.caption: BMI text
fields.tableau_formula:
IF [Calculation_549157724182274055] <= 18.4
THEN 'Underweight'
ELSEIF [Calculation_549157724182274055] <= 24.9
THEN 'Normal weight'
ELSE 'Overweight'
END

fields.caption: HR text
fields.tableau_formula:
IF [Latest BMI (copy)_549157724183056396] < 60 THEN
    'Low Heart Rate'
ELSEIF [Latest BMI (copy)_549157724183056396] >= 60 AND [Latest BMI (copy)_549157724183056396] <= 100 THEN
    'Normal Heart Rate'
ELSEIF [Latest BMI (copy)_549157724183056396] > 100 THEN
    'High Heart Rate'
ELSE
    'Invalid Heart Rate'
END

fields.caption: Latest Week
fields.tableau_formula: DATETRUNC('week', [Date]) = DATEADD('week', -1, DATETRUNC('week', { MAX([Date])} + 1))

    WHEN 'Last Week' THEN [Calculation_549157724167987201]

    WHEN 'Last Year' THEN [LC Month (copy)_549157724170584067]

children.text_html: Select Date Period to change view

DESIGNED AND DEVELOPED BY TOBILOBA BABAJIDEÆ
INSPIRED BY FIRMAN JP FOR VECTORAÆ

OVERVIEWÆ
The Health Tracker Dashboard is designed to help individuals monitor and manage their health metrics over a 2-year period, providing a comprehensive view of their daily health data. This tool visualizes various health indicators, allowing users to track changes and identify patterns over time. The dataset includes a range of health metrics generated to mimic real-life data distributions, ensuring realistic and meaningful insights.Æ

FIELD DETAILSÆ
The dataset spans from January 1, 2022, to December 31, 2023, comprising daily records of various health metrics. The columns in the dataset and their respective distributions are as follows:Æ
● Date: Date for each data entryÆ
● Weight (kg): Body weight measured in kilogramsÆ
● Height (cm): height measured in centimetersÆ
● BMI: Body Mass Index calculated from weight and heightÆ
● Calories Intake: Daily caloric consumption in caloriesÆ
● Calories Burned: Daily physical activity in caloriesÆ
● Sleep Duration (hours): Total number of hours spent sleepingÆ
● Steps Count: Daily number of steps takenÆ
● Heart Rate (bpm): Resting heart rate in beats per minuteÆ
● Blood Pressure Systolic (mmHg): Standard systolic blood pressure in mmHgÆ
● Blood Pressure Diastolic (mmHg): Standard diastolic blood pressure in mmHgÆ
● Mood Score (1-10): Daily mood assessment taking values between 1 and 10Æ
● Water Intake (liters): Total liters of water consumedÆ

INSPIRED BY FIRMAN JP FOR VECTORAÆ

theme.source_note: Tableau has no single global theme file - aggregated from per-worksheet mark-color formats; treat as a starting palette, not an authoritative theme to clone.

limitations_encountered.issue: extract-based (.hyper) data source - row data requires a separate extraction step (tableauhyperapi -> Parquet) or repointing to the true upstream system

Built local PBIP semantic model fabric/HealthTracker.SemanticModel: 2 tables (Health Data fact 730 rows import; Date Period disconnected slicer), 41 measures, 22 columns (13 real + 7 calc + 2 slicer). Deserializes clean via TmdlSerializer; measure names model-wide unique; no measure==same-table-column collision; all DAX bracket refs resolve.

10 FIXED-LOD 'Latest *' fields -> measures. DAX: VAR _d=[Latest Date] RETURN CALCULATE(AVERAGE('Health Data'[col]), FILTER(ALL('Health Data'),'Health Data'[Date]=_d)). AVG for all except Latest Water Intake=SUM (single-row grain so identical). Verified numerically against CSV max-date (2023-12-31) two independent ways (Tableau-semantics + DAX-mechanics replica), all agree: e.g. Latest BMI=22.86861, Latest Steps Count=13689.97, Latest Water Intake=2.05.

'Latest Date' bare-'{ MAX([Date]) }' implicit-FIXED LOD -> hidden helper measure CALCULATE(MAX('Health Data'[Date]),ALL('Health Data')), referenced by all Latest/Prior. PARSER GAP: bare '{ MAX(...) }' (no FIXED keyword) was NOT flagged is_lod=true (only '{FIXED...}' detected); also affects Prior Week/Month/Year and the inner max of Latest Week/Month/Year columns.

6 FIXED-LOD 'Prior *' fields -> measures, parameter-driven prior date via SWITCH([Date Period Value]): Last Week=maxDate-7, Last Month=EDATE(-1), Last Year=EDATE(-12). SUM for Cal Bur/Cal Int/Steps, AVG for Mood/Sleep/Water. Tableau 'Prior Week/Month/Year' datetime fields inlined into the _p VAR (not separate measures). Verified vs CSV for all 3 periods (e.g. Last Week 2023-12-24: Prior Steps Count=13289.64; Last Month 2023-11-30: Prior Cal Int=2312.75).

18 'Diff +/ - / No Diff' fields (6 metrics x 3) -> measures VAR _l=[Latest X] VAR _p=[Prior X] RETURN IF(_l>_p,_l-_p) / IF(_l<_p,...) / IF(_l=_p,...). Operands recovered via internal-name->caption substitution (raw formulas carry scrambled Ctrl-drag copy names, e.g. 'Latest HR bpm (copy)(copy)' actually resolves to 'Latest Steps Count'). Exact captions kept incl. +/- suffixes.

3 status-text fields -> measures over Latest values: BMI text SWITCH(<=18.4/<=24.9/else), HR text (<60/<=100/>100/else), BP text (AHA compound SWITCH(TRUE())). Verified: BMI text='Normal weight', HR text='Normal Heart Rate', BP text='Stage 1 Hypertension'. HR text final ELSE 'Invalid Heart Rate' is an unreachable dead branch (any real number hits one of the first three) - translated faithfully; likely source-workbook artifact.

Tableau parameter 'Date Period' (Last Week/Month/Year, default Last Week) -> disconnected single-column slicer table 'Date Period' + measure [Date Period Value]=SELECTEDVALUE(...,"Last Week"). Parameter-equality/slicer idiom; not a Field Parameter, not relationship-bound.

Tableau boolean field 'Date Period' (period membership filter) -> measure RENAMED to 'In Selected Period' to avoid collision with the 'Date Period' slicer table/column. Returns 1/0 per parameter-selected latest period. NOTE for report_build: a measure visual-filter restricts categories not raw bars - for exact per-bar row restriction, filter on the boolean calc columns 'Latest Week'/'Latest Month'/'Latest Year' directly (e.g. via bookmarks).

Tableau boolean 'Date Color' (color encoding) -> measure 'Highlight Latest' (1 highlights latest bar; Last Week/Month->Latest Day, Last Year->Latest Month). Tableau 'Label' field (IF [Date Color] THEN 'Yes') CONSOLIDATED into it - report shows data label where Highlight Latest=1.

Tableau 'Date period selected' (axis; DATENAME day for Week/Month, month for Year) -> two calc columns 'Day Of Month' (int) + 'Month Name' (string, sortByColumn Month Number). 'Date period selected name' (UPPER(month)) CONSOLIDATED into 'Month Name' (report uppercases at visual level).

4 boolean 'Latest Day/Week/Month/Year' -> calc COLUMNS (row-level, filter-independent via MAXX(ALL('Health Data'),[Date])). Faithful 'last COMPLETE period' semantics: Latest Week=Dec 24-30 2023 EXCLUDES maxDate Sun 2023-12-31 (Tableau DATETRUNC('week',maxDate+1)-1wk); Latest Month=Dec 2023 and Latest Year=2023 both INCLUDE maxDate. Verified membership from CSV.
Tableau 'Shape' field (constant 'S', shape-shelf marker) -> EXCLUDED from the semantic model (pure Tableau viz-encoding artifact; handled by PBI visual formatting).
'Health data.csv' data_type=table pseudo-column (Tableau relationship-model object-id anchor, internal_name [__tableau_internal_object_id__]...) -> EXCLUDED (not real data; already parser-flagged).

PARSER GAP (report for consolidation): Tableau internal field names are systematically scrambled by Ctrl-drag duplication (e.g. a Diff operand internally '[Latest HR bpm (copy)(copy)_...]' resolves to caption 'Latest Steps Count'). referenced_fields captures identity but NOT operand order/position; recovered by substituting each [internal_name] token with its resolved caption before translating. Recommend the parser emit a resolved/canonical formula.

REPORT-LAYER capability note: one Tableau parameter drives BOTH (a) period row-restriction AND (b) axis granularity swap (day<->month) on the 9 trend bars. Power BI cannot swap an axis column by a slicer selection - report_build must implement (b) via bookmarks/per-page filters selecting the correct boolean-column filter + axis field (Day Of Month for Week/Month, Month Name for Year). KPI tiles work directly off the slicer (Prior/Diff read [Date Period Value]).

Live in-engine EVALUATE not executed at semantic_build: environment has no headless ADOMD/query client (no ADOMD, pyadomd, pythonnet, or consumption skill) and two concurrent agent Power BI Desktop/msmdsrv instances (parallel batch) must not be disturbed. Numeric ground-truth done instead via two independent Python codings (Tableau-semantics + literal DAX-mechanics replica) agreeing to 1e-9 for all 10 Latest and 6x3 Prior values. First live-engine run deferred to report_build/validate against these documented expected values.

Source aggregation inconsistency: 'Latest Water Intake' uses SUM but 'Prior Water Intake' uses AVG in the Tableau formulas (single-row-per-date grain so numerically identical, =2.05 latest). Each translated faithfully to its own source aggregation rather than 'fixing' the mismatch.
